description:
You can also go and check out the old Japanese forest train at , where you can enter for free and stroll around, checking out the beautiful old locomotive before heading back to your accommodation.

A scenic park with a beautiful old locomotive offering free accessibility amidst nature.

The Taroko Gorge is filled with extreme nature, impossible architectural wizardry, and beautiful scenic hiking trails to the brim, all waiting for you to explore. There are also several scenic hiking trails you can partake in, such as the  that takes you to the Baiyang Waterfall and the beautiful Water Curtain Cave.

A scenic hike through extreme nature offers breathtaking views and unique architectural features, perfect for adventure seekers.

Places like the  (Eternal Spring Shrine), Zhuilu Old Trail (which requires a permit), and Lushui-Wenshan Trail are also worth visiting if you have an extra day in the park. Be sure to check if the trails are open before you embark on the journey. Sometimes, the trails may be closed due to hazardous conditions.

Nature-based attractions like hiking trails offer adventure opportunities with varying levels of accessibility and potential crowded conditions.

Spend a few hours there admiring the beauty of the temple before continuing on to explore other parts of Taipei, such as , where you will find a spacious courtyard, beautiful vegetation all around, and some stunning architecture like the Memorial Hall itself and the Liberty Square Arch.

A serene temple visit amidst nature's beauty precedes an exploration of cultural landmarks in Taipei.

To get to the National Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall, you will have to take the MRT Red or Green Line and get off at , where you can then take Exit 5 and you will be right at the side entrance of Liberty Square.

A crowded public transportation route to a cultural landmark.

From Chiayi, you will have to take the Alishan Shuttle Bus from  in front of Chiayi Station. The Chiayi-Alishan bus runs from 6:10 - 14:10, leaving every 30 minutes to 1 hour, and costs 240 TND per person. It will take you about 2 hours to get from Chiayi to Alishan.

A scenic journey by bus to Alishan, suitable for nature lovers, with a moderate cost of 240 TND per person.
After spending an hour at Eluanbi Park, make your way back to Kenting Town but right before you arrive, stop at  and check out this peculiar-looking rock. From the photo above, what do you see?

There are also 2 temples I recommend you visit: the Japanese-built  with a great view of the sea of clouds, and Shouzhen Temple, the largest temple in Alishan and the highest in terms of elevation in Taiwan.

A scenic temple visit offers breathtaking views of nature amidst cultural heritage.

Not too far from Xianglin Tree, you can continue on the walkway towards , a Japanese-built Buddhist temple with a killer lookout point to witness the sea of clouds.

In Asia, you can deep fry anything, and it will still taste delicious. That goes for the bucket of fried mushrooms I had at  in Hualien. Sold for about 70 NTD, you can get a bucket of fried mushrooms sprinkled with pepper and spice that you can indulge yourself in, just like a bucket of Kentucky Chicken 😀. If you are in Taiwan, be sure to look out for these delicious buckets of fried mushrooms.

A popular night market food offering affordable indulgence in nature's ingredients.

If you are not keen on paying 600 NTD to get a view of the city, the best alternative is to go a little further east and hike the famous  for a stunning view of Taipei 101 and the nightly cityscape of Taipei, free of charge!

A free hiking trail offers a stunning view of the city.

If you are not keen on paying 600 NTD to get the view of the city, the best alternative is to go a little further east and hike the famous , free of charge!

From there, simply climb up the stairs for about 20 minutes and you should arrive at the , where most people will stop. I recommend that you continue a bit further to Xiangshan Six Rocks, which should only take you no more than 5 minutes from the viewpoint, for a better view of Taipei.

A moderately accessible hike with scenic views, ideal for nature lovers and adventure seekers.

Another place I would recommend you stop by is . Within the park, there are several walking trails you can take. Some go through an open-air museum showing what the life of the indigenous people was like, while others take you to stunning viewpoints. All of them are worth visiting, so be sure to spend some time in the park.

A scenic park with accessible walking trails offering cultural and natural experiences amidst stunning views.

From Hualien, you can take the regular train from Hualien Station to  (one of the stops of the Kenting Express Bus), where you can then catch the Kenting Express Bus to Kenting National Park.

No romance or family mentioned, but a bus ride is involved.

After arriving at Fangliao Station, you will have to walk out to the main road and wait for the Kenting Bus at the , and you should be on your way in 30 minutes. From Fangliao to Kenting, the journey should take about 1 hour, depending on the traffic.

A bus ride from Fangliao to Kenting takes approximately 1 hour with potential delays due to traffic.

After admiring the stunning coastline of Kenting, continue north on your e-scooter for about 5 minutes and you will arrive at , a beautiful roadside beach area where you can stroll or, given that it's not too windy or cold, swim.
The trail begins at the  in Jiufen, and you will have to hike about 5.2 km for an hour before reaching Teapot Mountain. From the peak, it's another 20 minutes to Banpingshan.

Right around the corner from the Song of the Forest, you will find a little Japanese corner in Taiwan, the . The Hinoki Village is a small pocket of Japanese-style community where you will find plenty of Japanese restaurants, handicraft shops, a Japanese-style garden, and many photo opportunities if you are into photography. It's not very big, but it is a great place to kill some time while you are in Chiayi.

A small Japanese village with a serene garden, perfect for relaxation amidst nature, offering cultural experiences and photo opportunities.
After visiting Wenwu Temple, hop on another bus and make your way to  on the other side of the lake. Indulge in all of its street food at  Shopping District.

Yes, it's that sweet, and if you have a sweet tooth, do not forget to keep an eye out for one of these street food trucks while you are exploring a night market. I found this one at  at Sun Moon Lake.

Street food is available at night markets, offering affordable options amidst cultural exploration.

On your fourth day, we are going to go out of Taipei a bit and explore the beautiful town of Shifen, followed by a walk along the Spirited Away-inspired old streets of .

After half a day in Shifen, hop on a train back to Ruifang and take bus #960 or #788 from Ruifang Industrial Vocational High School bus stop to .

is a decommissioned gold mine town located on a mountain by the seaside north of Taipei. The town is now known for its night markets full of delicious food to try, the maze of small lanes and alleyways full of history, and the beautiful A-MEI Tea House that inspired some scenes in the movie Spirited Away.

I tried the Peanut Ice Cream at 3 night markets, one at the entrance of , one at Ningxia Night Market, and one at Shifen Train Station, all of which is extremely delicious. If you are looking for a sweet dessert to eat after a meal, be sure to look for the Peanut Ice Cream Roll.

The Peanut Ice Cream is extremely delicious and highly recommended as a post-meal treat.

Most of the attractions, cafes, and street food stalls in Jiufen are all concentrated on Shuqi Road, so once you arrive at the , continue up the road, and start exploring Jiufen Old Town from the small alleyway near 7-11.

From the bus stop, you can then take either bus #960 or #788, which should drop you off at . The bus will cost you 15 NTD and takes about 15 minutes.
Keep in mind that an e-scooter can only run along the coast. It doesn't have the horsepower to go up mountains, so you won't be able to ride up to  or hike Dejian Mountain.

Be sure to come back in time for you to return the scooter and enjoy all the awesome food at  along the main road before ending your day in Kenting.

starts from 6 PM until later in the night and is located along the side of the main road through Kenting Town. Here, you will find all sorts of delicious street food, seafood restaurants, and many other food stalls for you to indulge yourself in.

The Kenting Express operates from 8:30 - 19:10 every day, connecting Zuoying HSR Station to Kenting. It usually comes every 30 minutes, costs about 350 NTD, and takes about 2.5 hours to arrive. The bus can drop you off right in the center of Kenting at , and you should be able to walk to your accommodation.

A 2.5 hour bus ride from Zuoying HSR Station to Kenting for around 350 NTD, suitable for family travel.

One of the best photo spots in Taipei is at the  in Xinyi District near the Takemura Izakaya restaurant. This is the street where you can capture the iconic Taipei 101 between two buildings, creating the symmetrical art that you see above.

A scenic spot with a unique photo opportunity amidst a bustling city area.

If you still haven't had enough awesome photos from Taipei, I know the best place to capture the iconic Taipei 101. One of the best photo spots in Taipei is at the  in Xinyi District near the Takemura Izakaya restaurant.

It is a delicious combination of the light yet fulfilling rice used as bread and the sweet and delicious Taiwanese sausages that make this dish a must-try in Taiwan. I found the Taiwanese Sticky Rice Sausage at the lesser-known  in Taipei and at Kenting Night Market.

A delicious food option featuring Taiwanese sausages served with rice, often found at night markets.
If you have your own snorkeling gear or wish to swim a bit, the  is a beautiful hidden gem tucked away along the twisty road of Kenting's western section.

Take the bike path that goes along the lakeside, down through some of the most stunning viewpoints at Sun Moon Lake like  and Xiangshan Scenic Outlook, all the way to the Northwest Observation Platform.

A scenic bike path along a lake offers stunning natural views and cultural attractions.

First and foremost, on your first day in Kenting, rent an e-scooter, go out, and explore the coastline east of Kenting. Places like  and Fengchuisha are great places to begin your day, with a stroll along the coastline. Keep in mind, though, that the southern coastline of Taiwan sees extreme wind all year round, so be sure not to stand off a cliff.

Explore the coastline east of Kenting on an e-scooter for a fun adventure.

is an open grassland along the southern coastline of Taiwan, with a clear view of the Pacific Ocean. From the viewpoint, you will be able to see some of the most stunning coastlines in Taiwan, along with wild buffalos and vivid grassland.

is a Chinese temple built in the 18th century by Fujian immigrants from mainland China who came to the island and erected a temple on the spot. It is one of the most popular temples in Taiwan and has come to represent the pride of the Taiwanese people.

A historic temple representing Taiwanese culture, attracting many visitors.

Now that we have explored east of Kenting, it's time to go to the west. With your e-scooter, travel along the main road past Kenting Town and visit  for an epic view of the beautiful black limestone coastline, and lay down in the sun at White Sand Bay for the rest of the day.

A scenic bike ride or e-scooter trip offers a relaxing day by nature's beauty with family-friendly beaches.

Since you are already in the western section of Kenting, it would be a shame if you didn't visit , although I think the ticket is a little overpriced compared to other parks you can visit in Kenting.

To get to Little Bali Rock, you will have to ride west from Kenting Town to  and from there continue west until you arrive at another intersection. Go left and take Shawei Road down to Houbihu Harbor. Continue along the right side of the waterway until the road turns right, and that is where you will find Little Bali Rock.

If you are looking for a great place to watch the sunset, the  is a great place to do so. Its spacious courtyard, beautiful vegetation, and the elevated platform of the memorial hall will allow you to see the spanning view of Chiang Kai-shek Square against the backdrop of Liberty Square Arch and the beautiful sunset sky.

A scenic spot with beautiful vegetation for relaxation amidst a stunning natural setting.

The soft white steam bun of Xiao Long Bao is filled with tasty soup that you either have to drink from it or swallow it whole to truly do it justice. You can find Xiao Long Bao in most Taiwanese restaurants as well as from street food stalls in a night market such as  in Taipei and a night market in Kenting.

I tried the Peanut Ice Cream at 3 night markets, one at the entrance of Jiufen Old Street, one at , and one at Shifen Train Station, all of which is extremely delicious. If you are looking for a sweet dessert to eat after a meal, be sure to look for the Peanut Ice Cream Roll.

Taro Balls also come in deep-fried (why not?) and are filled with delicious fillings like salted egg and caramel. You can find one of these at  in Taipei, so be sure to give that a try too.
The trail will pass the , the Taiwanese Cypress that stands at 43.5m with a trunk circumference of 13.1m. It is estimated that the tree is over 2000 years old.

After half a day in Shifen, hop on a train back to  and take bus #960 or #788 from  Industrial Vocational High School bus stop to Jiufen.

To get from Taipei to Shifen, you will have to first take a train from Taipei Main Station to  via TRA (Taiwan Railways Administration), which should take around 42 minutes and cost 49 NTD per trip.

A relatively affordable train ride of 42 minutes for approximately $1.50.

On your fourth day, we are going to go out of Taipei a bit and explore the beautiful town of , followed by a walk along the Spirited Away-inspired old streets of Jiufen.

Another awesome day trip you can do from Taipei is a visit to , home to the mystical  Waterfall and Jiufen, a seaside old mountain village with streets full of lanterns, tea houses, and street food stalls for you to indulge yourself in.

A scenic day trip to Shifen offers natural beauty and cultural experiences amidst crowded streets.

What is there in Shifen, you asked? Well, , of course, which is considered to be one of the most scenic waterfalls in Taiwan. Its beautiful sheet of water flows down a 40m height rockface, creating a fog that helps mystify the forest around it.

A scenic waterfall surrounded by nature, ideal for adventure and relaxation amidst a misty atmosphere.

Another awesome day trip you can do from Taipei is a visit to Shifen, home to the mystical  and Jiufen, a seaside old mountain village with streets full of lanterns, tea houses, and street food stalls for you to indulge yourself in.

After walking around the Giant Trees Broadwalk, you will arrive at an opening where the beautiful  is located.  is the largest temple in Alishan and the highest in terms of elevation in Taiwan.

A serene temple surrounded by nature offers a peaceful escape amidst the natural beauty of Alishan.
After visiting Ci'en Pagoda, take the bus back to Shuishe, get your bike, and ride to  for a beautiful sunset before ending your incredible adventure at Sun Moon Lake.
If you don't mind waking up early, I highly recommend that you wake up early and watch the sunrise at  and walk by Meihe Park the next day.

Most of the attractions, cafes, and street food stalls in Jiufen are all concentrated on , so once you arrive at the Jiufen Old Town Bus Stop, continue up the road, and start exploring Jiufen Old Town from the small alleyway near 7-11.

Another place I would recommend you to check out is , one of the most photogenic places in Alishan with the saddest story ever!

After resting at Shouzhen Temple, you can continue east towards our next attraction, , one of the most photogenic places in Alishan. These two mountain ponds and the iconic pavilion are the perfect photo spot for those looking to capture a perfectly symmetrical photo of the ponds and their pavilion.

A scenic destination with natural beauty and cultural significance, ideal for photography and relaxation amidst nature.

After arriving in Chiayi, you can spend that evening exploring the city and checking out the , Hinoki Japanese Village, and Alishan Forest Railway Garage Park before heading over to Wenhua Road Night Market for a feast. This way, you can end the day and start early the next day to go to Alishan.

A cultural evening with local attractions followed by a crowded night market experience.

If you have a few hours to spare in Chiayi, you can go for a little stroll around the city and see what the city has to offer. One of the tourist attractions in Chiayi is the  art installation that looks like a bird's nest. It is a great place to relax and be around locals. It is also gorgeous at night, so be sure to swing by after sunset.
After visiting Fengchuisha, make your way back down along Jiae Road until you arrive at the , a monument built on a wooden platform overlooking Taiwan's rocky coast and the Pacific Ocean.

Another impressive scenic walking trail I would recommend you to do is the , a 1,372-meter-long walking trail that will take you through a narrow section of the Liwu River Gorge, allowing you to see some of the features of the gorge up close.

A scenic walking trail offering nature views and adventure opportunities amidst a natural setting.

There are several ways you can travel from Taipei to Hualien, but the easiest, most convenient way is to take the express train (TRA) from  to Hualien Railway Station.

From Sun Moon Lake, you can take Bus #1833 from the bus stop in front of the Tourist Information. It leaves 6 - 8 times a day from 7:30 to 16:20 every day. The journey should cost around 420 NTD per person, and you should arrive at  in 4 hours.

From Taoyuan International Airport, there are two ways you can travel to the city: by bus and by the Metro. The easiest option is to take bus #1819 from outside the Airport Terminal 2, which will take you to .
You can also visit the  and take a photo to commemorate the fact that you have finally traveled from the north all the way to the south of Taiwan.

From , there are two ways you can travel to the city: by bus and by the Metro. The easiest option is to take bus #1819 from outside the Airport Terminal 2, which will take you to Taipei Main Station.

A convenient public transportation system with affordable options for family travelers.

In order to get to Taroko Gorge from abroad, you will first have to travel to Taipei, and to do that, you will likely have to fly into  in Taipei. Thankfully, there are several airlines you can fly to Taipei.

If you have one more day in Taipei, I would recommend you come back to Jiufen and hike the  and Banpingshan Trail, two of the best hikes you can do in Northern Taiwan that will reward you with a stunning view of the northern coastline of Taiwan.

A scenic hike offers breathtaking views of nature and culture.
is a walking path that will take you through zig-zag trails that go around the Alishan forest, allowing you to enjoy the view while strolling safely on a wooden platform.

where the National Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall is located is full of beautiful architecture waiting for you to explore. The beautiful Liberty Square Arch at the northeast end of the square, the National Art Center and Theater on the South and North ends of the park respectively are some of the most beautiful architecture you will find in Taipei.

Beautiful architecture and cultural landmarks can be found in a crowded Liberty Square.

Along the Giant Trees Boardwalk #2, you will find one of the largest Taiwanese cypresses in the area, almost as big as the Xianglin Tree you saw previously.  stands at 43.5m with a trunk circumference of 13.1m. It is estimated that the tree is over 2,000 years old.

A massive ancient Taiwanese cypress offers a serene natural setting amidst a crowded boardwalk.

While you are there, also be sure to drop by , a beautiful western-style but Japanese-built department store turned creative marketplace that is promoting the locals' artistry and other cultural industries.

A luxurious spa offers a serene atmosphere for couples, while nearby The Red House promotes local culture and art.

After spending your morning admiring the beauty of Longshan, take the MRT Green Line and get off at Ximen Station where you will find a beautiful landmark of Taipei, .

is particularly my favorite. It is a hiking trail that will take you along the railroads before ascending mountains to the top of Tashan Peak, where you will be able to witness the Sea of Clouds and enjoy Alishan in solitude.

A scenic hike offering solitude amidst nature's beauty with stunning views of mountains and clouds.

A scenic hike offering solitude amidst nature's beauty.

After you are done, you will have to walk back the same way for about an hour before you arrive back in Tianxiang, which should be right on time for the third bus to depart at 10:40 from the .

A long walk is required after a visit, but it's accessible and convenient for getting back on schedule.

There is also the , the architectural wizardry that holds these treacherous roads together, preventing limestone mountains from collapsing upon them. There is a beautiful 30-minute scenic trail that hugs along the tunnel by the gorge, which I highly recommend.

A scenic trail offers breathtaking views and relaxation amidst nature's beauty.

There is no way to celebrate a productive day such as this than to devour all the street food at  in Chiayi. The night market is only a 15-minute walk from Chiayi Station, and the entire road turns into a night market with street food trucks along both sides of the road.

A casual night market setting offers affordable street food options to locals and visitors alike.

Fried Chicken Cutlet is becoming a popular snack among young people in Taiwan. It is basically a deep-fried chicken breast cutlet seasoned with powdery pepper and salt, giving it a texture you can't get in regular chicken. You can find these large fried chicken cutlets in any night market in Taiwan. I had a great one at  in Chiayi for only 100 NTD.
Take the #6669 bus from Sun Moon Lake Bus Stop and get off at  Bus Stop, and you will be right in front of our next destination, the massive .
After spending a few minutes at Maobitou Park, I would recommend you travel further west to , a beautiful strip of white sand beach where you can relax and enjoy the sunset.

From the Three Generation Tree, you can continue along the walking path to see , a 2,300-year-old Taiwan red cypress standing at 45m tall with a trunk circumference measuring at 12.3 meters. It is quite a sight to behold.

A natural wonder amidst a family-friendly setting, offering an opportunity for relaxation and scenic views.

To get to the Elephant trailhead, you will have to take the MRT Red Line to , take Exit 2 and walk south along the Xiangshan Park until the end of the road and then turn left and follow the road to the Xiangshan Trailhead.

Places to visit in Yangmingshan National Park are , a post-volcanic geological landscape area with a few scenic trails you can explore, Qixingshan (Seven Star Mountain) Trail, a 5.7 km long hiking trail that will take you to the top of Qixingshan Main Peak, and Erziping, a recreational area full of forests and mountains, etc.

A national park with scenic trails offering opportunities for adventure amidst nature's beauty.

To get to The Red House, you will have to take the MRT Green Line to  and take Exit 1, and you will be right at the entrance of The Red House.

To get to Ximending, you will have to take the MRT Green Line to  and take Exit 6 where you will be right at the entrance of the Ximending shopping street.

Crispy on the outside and soft on the inside, with all the sauces giving the dish a unique taste, you will forget about the smell the moment you try it. You can find them in almost any night market in Taiwan, such as the , etc.

A delicious street food experience offering a unique taste at an affordable price, often found in crowded night markets.

After the sunset, hop on an MRT and visit , one of the most popular shopping districts in Taipei. The entire neighborhood is filled with stores, street food stalls, and all kinds of entertainment you could imagine.

A crowded shopping district offers a lively atmosphere for adventure and culture amidst bustling streets and entertainment options.

A crowded shopping district offering a mix of culture, service, and entertainment options.

Although Ximending is not a full-blown night market like the others in Taipei, you will still find many iconic street foods here in the evening, as well as long lines of people waiting to buy the famous Boba tea of . So if you are looking for a place to try some local cuisine, be sure to drop by here at night.

A bustling area with iconic street food and crowded lines, ideal for trying local cuisine and experiencing Taiwanese culture.
After eating at Ita Thao, hop on another bus to  and hike up to Ci'en Pagoda for the most incredible view of Sun Moon Lake.

For your third day, you can hop on a bus and go and explore the wild nature of , which is home to Xiaoyoukeng Recreation Area, a post-volcanic geological landscape area with a few scenic trails you can do, Qixingshan (Seven Star Mountain) Trail, a 5.7 km long hiking trail that will take you to the top of Qixingshan Main Peak, and Erziping, a recreational area full of forest and mountains, and many others.

A scenic hike in Yangmingshan National Park offers nature and adventure amidst a post-volcanic landscape.

If you have a day to spare, I would recommend that you spend that day hiking around , a full-blown nature park filled with attractions and hiking trails that will occupy your entire day.

To get to Yangmingshan National Park, you will have to take the MRT Red Line to Jiantan Station, take Exit 1, and catch the bus at the bus stop on your left-hand side that is heading northward to Yangmingshan. You can take bus S15 or S17 from there to . The bus fare is 15 NTD, and the journey should take about 1 hour.

A scenic national park accessible by public transportation with a moderate cost of 15 NTD for a 1-hour journey.

From Sister Ponds, you will have to walk south to , and from there, you will find the Zhushan Sunrise Hiking Trail that will lead you through the forest. The trail merges with the road, and then you just have to follow the road until you arrive at Zhushan Station.

A scenic hiking trail through a forest offers an adventure amidst nature.

To get to Ningxia Night Market, you will have to take the MRT Green Line to , take Exit 5, and follow the road west until you arrive at Jiancheng Circle before turning right to the night market. The Ningxia night market opens from 5 PM to 1 AM, so be sure to go there at the right time.

A crowded night market with limited accessibility for those who are not familiar with local transportation systems.

If you like hiking, I would also recommend you to go hike the  for a grand view of Jade Mountain (Yushan), the highest mountain in Taiwan, and hike to Tashan Peak to witness the sea of clouds.

A scenic hiking trail offering breathtaking views of nature and culture, ideal for adventure seekers.

One of the best easy hikes you can do is to hike the  to the Zhushan Sunrise platform, even if it is not during sunrise. From the sunrise platform, you will be able to see Yushan, the highest mountain in Taiwan, and get a panoramic view of the surrounding area from the elevated platform.

The walk is easy, and it takes about 1 hour to complete one way. From Zhushan Station, you can go to  first to see the scenery from there, but for a better perspective, I would recommend you to walk another 10 minutes south towards Mt. Ogasawara, where the elevated viewing platform I mentioned earlier is located.

A leisurely 1-hour walk with scenic views of nature and culture.
After you get down from the escalator, all you have to do is wait for the yellow/white bus with the word "Kenting" plastered all over to arrive at  and get on it.

place: bus stop in front of the Tourist Information
description:
From Sun Moon Lake, you can take Bus #1833 from the . It leaves 6 - 8 times a day from 7:30 to 16:20 every day. The journey should cost around 420 NTD per person, and you should arrive at Taipei Main Station in 4 hours.

A 4-hour bus ride from Sun Moon Lake to Taipei Main Station costs approximately 420 NTD per person.
If you wish to go beyond this guide and visit some of the hard-to-reach locations, I would recommend you rent a car from a . It should cost you about 1800 NTD to rent a car for a day.

You can find plenty of food trucks selling these, but one of the best ones you can find is at the  in Taipei, where you can choose which toppings you want (100 - 150 NTD). Additionally, you will get a discount for drinks if you order them from the drink shop next door.

A food truck offering customizable options with affordable prices, ideal for families and those on a budget.

The bus will drop you off  by the entrance of the park, where you can prepare snacks and get ready for a full-day adventure in Alishan. After you are done, you can proceed to the gate, pay the 150 NTD entrance fee, and you should get an English map that you can follow to proceed to our first attraction, the Three Generation Tree.

The bus leaves  where they dropped you off upon your arrival. You can buy the bus ticket inside 7-11 or use EasyCard as I did. You should return to Chiayi by 7 PM, just in time for the night market.

A day of adventure awaits with a moderate entrance fee, accessible via public transportation.

To get to the southernmost point of Taiwan, simply drive back from Longpan Park and just before the road u-turns itself to go back up the other side of the coast (), continue south along the road until you find a large parking lot. From there, you can walk about 500m until you reach the monument.

place: one of the first few restaurants when you enter Jiufen Old Town
description:
Its soft, spongy taro balls, together with the mild sweetness of the syrup and flour, create one of the most iconic desserts in Taiwan. You can find Taro Balls at  or you can find them in most night markets in Taipei.

Traditional Taiwanese dessert often found in night markets, offering a sweet treat amidst cultural heritage and natural scenery.

A traditional Taiwanese dessert enjoyed by locals and tourists alike, often found in crowded night markets.
If you plan to spend an entire day in the park, I would recommend that you buy the Taroko Day Pass from the kiosk inside the  before getting on the bus.

place: rental place at the corner of Tonghai Lane and Kenting Main Road
description: I rented an e-scooter from a  for 450 NTD for 12 hours. The e-scooter comes with 2 batteries, which you can switch yourself or have the store do it for you.

The journey should take about an hour before you arrive at Tianxiang Recreational Area, the terminal bus stop inside Taroko National Park. From there, you can start your journey by doing the Baiyang Scenic Walk which begins from the

A scenic walk amidst nature is part of an adventure in a national park with accessible trails.
Along the , you will find many street food stalls and restaurants. So, you can also have breakfast or lunch here before heading out to Jiufen.

place: to Zuoying HSR Station (Xin Zuoying Station)
description:
From Kenting, you can take the morning (8 AM is the first one) Kenting Express Bus back  and then you can take the one-hour train to Chiayi. It should cost about 145 - 220 NTD depending on the duration.

A budget-friendly option for travel, with costs ranging from 145 to 220 NTD.
